L1.area: Binary program loading, relocation, and execution setup

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where values read directly from untrusted binary input are used in arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, division, and modulo) without overflow checks. When a crafted input file contains unusually large address or symbol values, integer overflow occurs during address and offset calculations, potentially leading to incorrect memory access or program misbehavior.

L3.area: ELF binary relocation and symbol resolution logic
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the ELF processing routines where symbol values (such as st_value) read directly from untrusted ELF input are used in unchecked addition with computed physical addresses during relocation. When a crafted ELF file contains unusually large symbol values, integer overflow occurs during physical address calculation, potentially leading to incorrect memory writes. Additional unchecked arithmetic in entrypoint offset computation, instruction index calculation, and section length accumulation are also affected.

ground_truth.reason: Multiple unchecked integer arithmetic operations in ELF parsing and relocation allow a crafted ELF file with large symbol or address values to trigger integer overflow, leading to incorrect address calculations and potential memory corruption.
